mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on future equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, so a persistent easing trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS declines for two consecutive readings, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in housing starts indicates improving household demand and construction activity, which feeds into broader earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher 60-day forward SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the trigger has negative average returns over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and consumption, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX posts positive 20-day returns in most instances after the trigger, or if the trigger's conditional Rank IC is not negative in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and industrial production both expand, the signal indicates broad-based real activity and revenue growth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone for 20-day returns, or if the combined signal has zero or negative conditional mean return over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low FEDFUNDS rate combined with subdued CPI inflation indicates real rates are falling and policy is accommodative, supporting equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered state does not generate positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if returns are not superior to periods with low rates but high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in payroll employment and rise in unemployment captures a true labor-market contraction, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 straight months while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor contraction signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not negative in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in real GDP relative to prior readings signals contracting aggregate demand and weaker corporate revenue prospects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-over-quarter GDP declines are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal's conditional mean return is non-negative over a rolling 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is falling while consumer prices remain elevated, the mix implies supply stress and margin compression rather than clean demand growth, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns when INDPRO trends down and CPI remains elevated, or if the combined effect is indistinguishable from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.
